entries.title: Homily on Acts 53 on acts 27:3
entries.excerpt: "And Julius courteously entreated Paul, and gave him liberty to go unto his friends to refresh himself. Julius gave Paul liberty," it says, acting "courteously, that he might refresh himself;" as it was but natural that he should be much the worse from his bonds and the fear, and the being dragged hither and thither. See how the writer does not hide this either, that Paul wished "to refresh himself."

entries.title: CATENA ON THE ACTS OF THE APOSTLES 27.1-3 on acts 27:3
entries.excerpt: Aristarchus accompanies Paul for a while. He is fittingly and usefully present with the intention of reporting everything concerning him in Macedonia.

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 27:3
entries.excerpt: "Julius, treating Paul kindly." And it was natural to lead and support Paul, who was weighed down by chains and fear. Notice how the writer does not conceal the fact that Paul was in need of care for himself.
